name: General Digital Work Product
professionalExpectations: Translate the owner outcome into a concrete, reviewable deliverable instead of stopping at planning notes.
Keep assumptions explicit and make the smallest professional product that satisfies the requested outcome.
Produce files that can be opened, inspected, and validated by the owner.
Keep credentials, customer data, production data, paid actions, deployments, messaging, posting, and DNS outside the build unless separately approved.
minimumQualityBar: The requested outcome is represented by owner-usable files.
No placeholder-only output is presented as complete.
The result includes a clear entry point or opening instruction.
Quality and lesson evidence is recorded before the job becomes done.
commonModules: Approval-gated integration notes when external actions are requested
uxExpectations: The owner can understand what was produced without reading internal AG OS records.
The primary result is reachable in one action from the job or workspace.
Errors and incomplete states are shown as blocked or failed, never as completed.

components: Use the simplest local file format or application stack that fits the requested outcome.
Prefer existing project tooling when a registered project is targeted.
Keep source in an isolated workspace until repository or deployment actions are separately approved.
recommendedStack.notes: This fallback enables safe planning and building for legitimate product types that do not yet have a specialized archetype. It does not authorize external actions.

phases.name: Understand the owner outcome
goals: Identify the concrete deliverable, intended user, and acceptance conditions.
Select the smallest appropriate file or application shape.

phases.name: Build the local work product
goals: Create complete owner-usable files inside the isolated workspace.

phases.name: Verify and explain
goals: Record the entry point, quality score, and lesson candidates.

knownPitfalls: Treating a planning document as the finished product.
Inventing a specialized stack before the deliverable shape is clear.
Marking a job done when ownerUsable is false.
Crossing into live services because the requested product mentions them.
approvalGates: Repository creation or mutation requires its exact connector approval.
Deployment, production data, credentials, messaging, posting, paid actions, and DNS each require separate exact approval.
qualityChecklist: The result directly answers the owner command.
At least one owner-usable file exists.
The entry point is identified and opens correctly.
No secret or prohibited data is present.
Incomplete work is not labeled done.
whatToBuildFirst: A clear entry point and acceptance checklist.
Local validation before external integration planning.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Infrastructure not required by the owner outcome.
External connectors before the local product is reviewable.
Production deployment or data paths before exact approval.
stopConditions: Stop before any credential, customer data, production data, paid action, deployment, message, post, or DNS change without exact approval.
Stop if the requested outcome cannot be represented as owner-usable files or requires a missing owner decision.
notes: Generic fallback knowledge only. Specialized archetypes remain preferred when a deterministic match exists, and this record grants no permission.
